EnglishDescription: What else could be a greater merit for this Ziyarat than to be the ’Word of Allah’ (Hadees’e Qudsi)? No other Ziyarat bears this eminence and superiority. The words from Allah (swt) other than the verses of Qur’an are said to be Hadees’e Qudsi. Safwan (r) narrates that Jibraeel (a) conveyed this Ziyarat to Holy Prophet (s) and through Imam Baqir (a) it reached to the public. (Shifa al-Sudoor, pg.51 by Abul Fazl Tehrani)
RUrduDescription: Isse badh kar kya fazilat ho sakti hai, keh yeh ziyarat, Allah (swt) ka kalaam (Hadees’e Qudsi) hai? Koi ziyarat itni ehmiyat aur buland maqaam nahi rakhti. Qur’an ki aayato’n ke alaawa jo bhi Allah (swt) ka kalaam hai usey Hadees’e Qudsi kehte hai. Safwan (r) se riwaayat hai keh, Jibraeel (a) ne Rasool-e-Khuda (s) ko yeh ziyarat pohonchaaya, aur Imam Baqir (a) ke zariye logo’n tak yeh ziyarat pohonchi hai. (Shifa al-Sudoor, pg.51 by Abul Fazl Tehrani)

EnglishDescription: Marhoom Shaikh Jawad ibne Shaikh Mashkoor, was a leading jurist of Najaf who died at the age of 90 years in 1337 H. He is buried next to his father in a room in the blessed courtyard of the grave of Ameerul Momineen (a). This great scholar dreamt in the night of 26 Safar 1332 H. in which he saw Izraeel (a) (the angel of death). He did salaam to the angel of death and asked him from where he was coming. The angel of death replied, ’I am coming from Shiraz after having seized the soul (Rooh) of Mirza Ibrahim Mehlaaki.’ Shaikh asked him ’In what condition is his soul in Barzakh?’
RUrduDescription: Marhoom Shaikh Ibne Shaikh Mashkoor, jo Najaf mein apne waqt ke aalam thay, unki wafat 1337H mein huwi jab woh 90 saal ke thay. Woh apne waalid ke paas ek kamre mein Imam Ali (a) ke haram mein dafn kiye gaye. Yeh buzurg aalim ne 26 Safar 1332 H. mein ek khwaab dekha, jis mein unho ne Izraeel (a) (maut ke farishte) ko dekha. Unho ne maut ke farishte ko salaam kiya aur pooncha keh, “aap kahan se aa rahe ho?” Maut ke farishte ne kaha, “Mai Shiraz se aa raha hu, Mirza Ibrahim Mehlaaki ki rooh kabz kar ke.” Shaikh ne pooncha keh, Barzakh mein unki rooh kis haal mein hai?”

EnglishDescription: The angel of death replied ’He is residing in the most beautiful garden and is in the best of the conditions. Allah has appointed 1,000 angels at his beck and call.’ Shaikh asked him ’For which action has he received such a great reward, for his high level of knowledge? Or for the large number of students he had trained?’ The angel of death replied, ’For none of these reasons’. Shaikh asked, ’Then for leading the congregational prayers and explaining the religious laws to the people?’ The angel of death replied in the negative. So Shaikh asked, ’Then on what basis did he receive such a great position?’ The angel of death said ’For reciting Ziyarate Ashura.’ Mirza Mehlaaki recited Ziyarate Ashura regularly in the last 30 years of his life. And if due to an illness or any other reason, he was unable to recite the Ziyarat, then he would appoint a representative to recite the Ziyarat on his behalf.’
RUrduDescription: Maut ke farishte ne kaha, “Woh behtareen baghiche mein araam farma rahe hai aur achchhi haalat mein hai. Allah (swt) ne 1000 farishto’n ko unki khidmat mein lagaya hai.” Shaikh ne pooncha “Kis amal ki wajah se unhe yeh maqaam haasil huwa hai, unke ilm ke meyaar par? Ya phir unho ne badi tadaad mein tullaab (students) ki tarbiyat ki, us bina par?” Maut ke farishte ne farmaya, “nahi.” Shaikh ne pooncha, “phir kis bina par unhe yeh maqaam haasil huwa?” Maut ke farishte ne kaha, “Ziyarate Ashura padhne par. Mirza Mehlaaki musalsal apni zindagi ke aakhri 30 saal Ziyarate Ashura padhte rahe. Jab woh bimaar ya kisi aur wujuhaat se nahi padh paate, to kisi ko apni niyaabat mein padhwaate thay.”

EnglishDescription: The next morning, Shaikh Jawad went to Ayatullah Mirza Mohammed Taqi Shirazi and narrated his dream. On hearing this, Ayatullah Mirza Mohammed Taqi began weeping. When people asked him the reason of his grief, he replied ’Mirza Ibrahim Mehlaaki was a pillar of jurisprudence and he has departed from the world.’ People said ’But this news was only a dream, while we don’t know the fact as yet.’ Ayatullah Mirza Taqi replied, ’This was not the dream of an ordinary individual, it was the dream of Shaikh Jawad.’ The very next day there was letter informing the residents of Najaf about Mirza Ibrahim Mehlaaki’s demise. In this way, the veracity of Shaikh Jawad’s dream proved to be true. (Ziyarate Ashura by Nasir Rasti Lahejaani, pg.27). This incident highlights the significance of reciting Ziyarate Ashura regularly.
RUrduDescription: Dusri subh ko, Shaikh Jawad ne, Ayatullah Mirza Muhammad Taqi Shirazi ko apna khwaab bayaan kiya. Yeh soonne par Ayatullah Taqi girya karne lage. Jab logo’n ne girya ka sabab pooncha, to unho ne kaha keh, Mirza Ibrahim Mehlaaki ishtehaad ke sutoon (pillar) thay aur woh is duniya se guzar gaye.” Logo’n ne kaha “magar yeh khabar to sirf ek khwaab hai, jab keh haqeeqat kya hai hamein maaloom nahi.” Ayatullah Taqi ne farmaya, “Yeh khwaab kisi aam insaan ka nahi, magar yeh keh Shaikh Jawad ka hai.” Dusre hi din Najaf waalo’n ke liye ek khat aaya jis mein Mirza Ibrahim Mehlaaki ki maut ka paighaam tha. Is tarah Shaikh Jawad ke khwaab ki sachchaayi saabit huwi. (Ziyarate Ashura by Nasir Rasti Lahejaani, pg.27). Is waqye se Ziyarate Ashura ki ehmiyat ka pata chalta hai.

EnglishTitle: Consequences and benefits of Ziyarat’e Ashura…
RUrduTitle: Ziyarat’e Ashura ke asaraat aur uske faaiyde…

EnglishDescription: Shaheed’e Mehraab, Ayatullah Dastghaib Shirazi (r) narrates an incident from Ayatullah Uzma Shaikh Abdul Kareem Haayeri (r), the founder of the Hauz’e Ilmiyyah in Qum. Janab Haayeri (r) narrates, ’I was residing in Samarrah to pursue religious studies. Once, the city was struck by a deadly plague. Scores of people were dying every day. During those days, a group of religious scholars had gathered at the house of our teacher late Syed Mohammed Fesharki (r). At that time, Janab Mirza Mohammed Taqi (r) too came in that meeting. The talk turned towards the topic of plague and the devastation it was causing in terms of loss of lives.
RUrduDescription: Shaheed’e Mehraab, Ayatullah Dastghaib Shirazi, riwaayat karte hai Ayatullah Uzma Shaikh Abdul Kareem Haayeri se, jo Hauze Ilmiyyah Qum ke baani hai. Shaikh Haayeri farmate hai, “Mai Samarrah mein deeni uloom haasil kar raha tha. Ek baar Samarrah mein waba (bimaari) phaili jis se log marne lage. Achchi tadaat mein rozana maut hoti thi. Us waqt Ulma ka ek giroh hamare ustaad Marhoom Syed Muhammad Fesharki ke ghar jama huwe. Us waqt Mirza Muhammad Taqi bhi aaye huwe thay. Ghufatgu ke dauraan is bimaari aur uske asaraat par charcha hone lagi, keh kis tarah logo’n ki jaan is bimari ki wajah se ja rahi hai.”

EnglishDescription: Marhoom Mirza Taqi (r) said, "If I command you to perform an action will all of you obey me?" Everyone replied "Yes, of course, we will obey whatever you say." Marhoom Mirza (r) then ordered "Tell every Shia of Samarrah to recite Ziyarate Ashura for the next 10 days and to gift the reward of the Ziyarat to the soul of the mother of Imam’e Zamana (a), Janabe Nargis Khatoon (s) so that the people remain safe from this disease." All those who were present in that gathering passed this information to the Shias of Samarrah, who all began reciting Ziyarate Ashura. From the very next day, there was no news of any Shia death. Only the news of the death of the Ahle Sunnat was being reported. Gradually the Sunnis realized this and asked some of their Shia friends as to the reason that why none from the Shias were dying of plague. The Shias said that they were reciting Ziyarate Ashura. On hearing this, the Sunnis too began reciting the Ziyarat. Consequently, they too were secured from the perilous effects of the plague.’ (Ziyarate Ashura - Effects & Miracles, p. 24)
RUrduDescription: Marhoom Mirza Taqi (r) ne kaha, “Agar mai tumhe koi amal karne ka hukm du, to kya aap log meri baat maano-ge?” Sabhi ne kaha, “Be-shak jo aap kahenge usey ham qabool karenge.” Marhoom Mirza (r) ne kaha, “Samarrah ke har Shia se kahe do, keh aane waale 10 dino tak Ziyarate Ashura padhe aur usey Imam’e Zamana (a) ki waalida, Janabe Nargis Khaatoon (s) ko hadiya kare, ta-keh log is bimaari se mehfooz rahe. Har koi jo is nashisht (meeting) mein haazir tha usne logo’n tak yeh hukm pohonchaaya, jisse logo’n ne padhna shuru kiya. Dusre hi din se ek bhi Shia ki maut ki khabar nahi aayi. Sirf Ahle Sunnat hazraat ki maut ho rahi thi. kuchh dino ke baad Sunni hazraat ko yeh ehsaas huwa, to unho ne apne Shia dosto’n se iski wajah poonchi, keh kyu ab ek bhi Shia ki maut is bimaari se nahi ho rahi hai? Shiyo’n ne kaha keh ham rozana Ziyarate Ashura padhte hai. Iske baad se woh log bhi Ziyarate Ashura padhne lage aur dhire dhire woh bhi is waba se mehfooz ho gaye.” (Ziyarate Ashura - Effects & Miracles, p. 24)

EnglishTitle: Imam’e Zamana (a) Emphasis for Ziyarat’e Ashura…
RUrduTitle: Ziyarat’e Ashura ke liye Imam’e Zamana (a) ki taaqid…
UrduTitle: زیارتِ عاشورا کے لیئے امام زمانہ (ع) کی تاکید۔۔۔

EnglishDescription: Shaikh Abbas Qummi (r) in Mafaateeh al-Jenan has mentioned an incident from his teacher, Janab Mirza Husain Tabarsi (r), famous as Mohaddis Noori (r), where Imam’e Zamana (a) has emphasized the regular recitation of Ziyarate Ashura.
RUrduDescription: Shaikh Abbas Qummi apni kitaab, Mafatih al-Jenan mein apne ek ustaad ka waqya likhte hai, Janab Mirza Husain Tabarsi, jo mashhoor hai Mohaddis Noori ke naam se, jis waaqye mein Imam’e Zamana (a) ne Ziyarate Ashura padhne ki taaqid ki hai.
UrduDescription: ہمارے استاد محترم نے نجم الثاقب میں ایک واقعہ بیان کیا ہے جس سے ظاہر ہوتا ہے کہ اس زیارت کو ہمیشہ پابندی کے ساتھ پڑھتے رہنا چاہیے اور اس میں غفلت نہیں کرنی چاہیے۔

EnglishDescription: This incident pertains to Janab Syed Ahmed bin Syed Hashim bin Syed Hasan Musawi Rashti. He was a trader from the city of Rasht. He says, In the year 1280 H, I left the city of Rasht towards the city of Tabriz, intending to go on the ritual pilgrimage (hajj). I resided in the house of Ali al-Tabrizi, the famous merchant, and had to stay there with bewilderment, because I could not find a caravan to join. Hajj Jabbar al-Sadahi al-Isbahani, the pioneer, prepared for a caravan to the city of Tarabuzn; I therefore hired a riding animal from him and joined the caravan alone, without having any friend with me. In the first station of our journey, three men joined me after Hajj Jabbar had arisen their desires to accompany me. These three men were Baqir al-Tabrizi, Sayyid Husayn al-Tabrizi, a merchant, and Ali who was a servant. During the journey, we accompanied each other up to the city of Arzanat al-Rum from which we set for Tarabuzn.
RUrduDescription: Yeh waaqya Janab Syed Ahmed ibne Syed Hashim ibne Syed Hasan Musawi Rashti ke silsil mein hai. Woh Rasht naami shaher ke taajir (businessman) thay. Woh farmate hai keh, “Saal 1280H mein mai Rasht shaher chhod kar Tabriz gaya, yeh niyyat se keh Hajj par jaawu. Mai Ali Tabrizi jo ek mashhoor taajir thay unke ghar par muqaam kiya. Mai us waqt pareshaan tha keh koi Hajj ka kaafilah nahi tha jis ke saath mai Hajj ko rawaana hota. Jabbar al-Sadahi al-Isbahani jo Carvaan ke saalaar thay unho ne ek kaafila tayyar kiya jo shaher Tarabuzn ke liye ja raha tha. To mai ne ek sawaari kiraaye par li aur us khaafileh ke saath rawaana huwa, jab keh mera koi bhi saathi nahi tha. Pehle maqaam par jahan ham ruke waha se 3 log khaafileh mein shaamil huwe jinka naam Baqir al-Tabrizi, Sayyed Husain al-Tabrizi, jo taajir tha, aur Ali jo keh naukar tha. Safar mein ham ek-dusre ke saath thay keh ham Arzanat al-Rum pohonche keh jahan se hamein Tarabuzn pohonchna tha.
UrduDescription: یہ واقعہ جناب سید احمد ابن ہاشم ابن سید حسن موسوی رشتی ایک تاجرتھے جو رشت میں رہتے تھے۔ ان سید محترم نے فرمایا : میں؁ ۱۲۸۰ھ میں حج بیت اللہ کے قصد سے اپنے شہر رشت سے تبریز آیا اور وہاں کے معروف تاجر حاجی صفر علی تبریزی کے گھر میں قیام کیا وہاں مجھے حج کو جانے والا کوئی قافلہ نہیں مل رہا تھا۔ لہٰذا میں بہت پریشان خاطر تھا اسی اثنا میں حاجی جبار اصفہانی آ گئے جو سامان لے کر طرابوزن کی طرف جا رہے تھے چنانچہ ان سے سواری کرایہ پر لے کر ہم بھی ان کے ساتھ چل پڑے جب ہم پہلی منزل پر اترے تو حاجی صفر علی تبریزی کی ترغیب و تشویق سے تین اور آدمی وہاں سے ہمارے ساتھ آملے ان میں سے ایک تو حاجی ملا باقر تبریزی تھے، دوسرے حاجی سید حسین تبریزی جوتا جرتھے اور تیسرے حاجی علی تھے۔ چنانچہ ہم چاروں قافلے کے ہمراہ روانہ ہو کروہاں سے طرابوزن کی طرف گامزن ہوئے۔

EnglishDescription: In one of the stations between the city Arzanat al-Rum and Tarabuzn, Hajj Jabbar the pioneer approached and informed us that we would soon pass through a frightening way; therefore, we must not lag behind the caravan. Complying with his warning, we hurried up. Two hours and a half or three hours before dawn, we resumed our journey altogether. No more than half a parasang walking or little more, it became dark and snow began falling down, making each one of us cover the head with every coverlet we had and try to proceed as quickly as possible. As to me, I was unable to catch them; therefore, I rode off and sat to a side of the way with exhaustive confusion. As I thought of the matter a lot, I deemed proper to stay in my place up to dawning, because the journey expenditure, which was six hundred Tumans, was entirely with me. Then, I would return to the house in which we had stayed last night and then come back again with some guards to join the caravan.
RUrduDescription: Carvaan ke saalaar ne hamein bataaya ke Arzanat al-Rum aur Tarabuzn ke darmiyaan mein ek khatarnaak raaste se tezi se guzarna hai, to yeh khayaal rahe keh koi bhi kaafileh se bichhad na jaaye. Is baat ko dhyaan mein rakhte huwe ham log tezi se tayyar huwe aur subh sadiq (waqt Namaze Subh) se 2.5 ya 3 ghante (hours) pehle rawaana huwe. Ab thodi hi dur nikle thay, keh achaanak aasmaan shiya (kaala) ho gaya aur barf (snow) girne laga, jisse ham sab logo’n ne apne sar ko dhaank liya aur tezi se manzil ki taraf badh ne lage. Is darmiyaan mai kaafileh se bichhad gaya, jiski wajah se mai ek kinaare baiyt gaya. Mai kaafi der sochta raha, keh kis taraf meri manzil hai, magar mujhe kuchh sujaayi nahi diya, mai ne yeh taiy kiya keh subh saadiq hone tak is maqaam par thaiyr jaau is liye keh jo safar ke akhrajaat 600 Tumaan thay woh saari rakam mere paas thi. Mai ne socha keh mai laut jaau aur phir dusre kaafileh ke saath dubaara jaawu.
UrduDescription: ان دونوں شہروں کے درمیان کی ایک منزل پر قافلہ سالار حاجی جبارجلودار ہمارے پاس آئے اور کہا : آگے کی منزل بہت پُر خطر ہے لہٰذا آج ذرا جلدی تیار ہو جانا تاکہ آپ یہ منزل قافلے کے ہمراہ طے کر لیں، اس سے پہلے ہم چاروں ساتھی قافلے کے پیچھے کچھ فاصلے پر چلتے آ رہے تھے۔

EnglishDescription: Suddenly, I noticed that there was an orchard in front of me and there was a peasant catching a shovel in the hand by which he stroke the leaves of the trees to make the amassing snow fall down. The man approached me and asked, “Who are you?” “I have lagged behind the caravan and I am now lost,” I answered.
RUrduDescription: Ittefaaq se mai ne yeh dekha keh mere aaghe ek peyd (tree) hai aur ek shakhs jiske haath mein ek kulaadi hai jisse us ne peyd par maar kar barf (snow) ko giraya. Woh shakhs mujh se kaha, “Tum kaun ho?” Mai ne kaha, “Mai apne kaafileh se bichhad gaya hu aur apna raasta bhul gaya hu.”
UrduDescription: حاجی جبار کے کہنے پر ہم آج بہت جلد تیار ہوئے اور طلوع فجر سے تقریباً تین گھنٹے قبل سفر شروع کر دیا، ابھی ہم تین ہی میل آگے بڑھے ہوں گے کہ بادل گِھر گئے اور برف باری بھی ہونے لگی، چنانچہ قافلے والوں میں سے ہر ایک نے تیزی سے چلنا شروع کر دیا۔

EnglishDescription: Speaking in Persian language, the man said, “You are advised to offer the Namaz Shab so that you will be guided to the right way.” Following his advise, I began offering Namaz Shab. When I accomplished the early dawn acts of worship, the man came to me again and asked, “Have you not left yet?” “In fact,” I said, “I cannot find the way.”
RUrduDescription: Faarsi mein bolte huwe us shakhs ne kaha, “Tumhe nasiyyat karta hu, keh tum Namaze Shab adaa karo, ta-keh tumhari sahih rehnumaayi ho.” Uski baat par mai ne amal kiya aur Namaze Shab adaa kiya. Jab mai ne Namaz tamaam ki, to phir se woh shakhs aaya aur usne kaha, “Tum ab tak gaye nahi?” To mai ne kaha keh “mujhe raasta nahi maaloom.”
UrduDescription: میں نے ان کے برابر چلنے کی بڑی کوشش کی لیکن میں ایسا نہ کر سکا اور وہ لوگ بہت دور نکل گئے اور میں ان سے بچھڑ گیا۔ میں گھوڑے سے اتر کر سرِراہ ایک طرف بیٹھ گیا۔

EnglishDescription: The man advised again, “You may say the Major Comprehensive Form of Ziyarat (Jame’a Kabir).” Although I did not memorize this form of Ziyarat and, even now, I cannot say it by heart although I have very frequently visited the holy shrines, at that moment I stood up and said the entire form of that ziyarat by heart.
RUrduDescription: Us shakhs ne phir se kaha, “Tum Ziyarate Jame’a Kabir padho’n.” Mujhe woh ziyarat yaad nahi thi, jab keh mai musal-sal ziyarat par jaaya karta tha. Us waqt mai khada huwa aur puri ziyarat ko bagair dekhe padh liya.
UrduDescription: جب کہ گھبراہٹ اور پریشانی مجھ پر چھائی ہوئی تھی، کیوں کہ سفرکے خرچ کی چھ سو تومان کی رقم میرے پاس تھی میں نے سوچا کہ سورج نکلنے تک یہاں بیٹھا رہوں اور پھر پچھلی منزل پر جاؤں جہاں سے آج ہم روانہ ہوئے تھے‘ میں چاہتا تھا کہ اس منزل سے چند محافظ ساتھ لے کر قافلے سے جاملوں گا۔

EnglishDescription: When I finished, the man appeared to me again and asked, “Have you not left your place yet?” Weeping forth, I said, “No, I have not left my place yet, because I cannot find the way.” The man advised again, “You may say Ziyarat Ashura.”
RUrduDescription: Jab ziyarat se faarigh huwa to woh shakhs phir se aaya aur kaha, “Ab tak aap yahan se gaye nahi?” Girya karte huwe mai ne kaha, “Nahi, mai abhi tak yahan se ja nahi paaya, keh mujhe raasta nahi maaloom.” Us shakhs ne mujhe kaha keh, “Tum Ziyarate Ashura padho’n”
UrduDescription: اس دوران میں نے دیکھا کہ سامنے ایک باغ ہے اور اس کا مالی بیلچہ لئے درختوں پر گری ہوئی برف ہٹا رہا ہے تھوڑی دیر کے بعد وہ میرے قریب آ کر کھڑا ہو گیا اور فارسی زبان میں پوچھا کہ تم کون ہو؟ میں نے کہا: میرے قافلے والے آگے نکل گئے ہیں اور میں ان سے جدا ہو کر یہاں رہ گیا ہوں، جب کہ میں ان راستوں سے واقف نہیں ہوں۔

EnglishDescription: Although I did not memorize this form of ziyarat either and even now I cannot say it by heart, at that very moment I stood up and said the entire form of Ziyarat Ashura’ by heart following it with the statements of invoking curse on the enemies and invoking peace on the Imam (a) and the dua known as Alqamah.
RUrduDescription: Jab-keh mujhe yaad nahi thi yeh ziyarat, us waqt mai khaad huwa aur poori Ziyarat’e Ashura aur Dua Alqamah bhi bagair dekhe padhli.
UrduDescription: اس بزرگ نے فرمایا: اتنے میں نماز تہجد ادا کرو تاکہ تمہیں راستہ معلوم ہو جائے میں نا فلۂ شب ادا کرنے لگا اور وہ چلے گئے،جب میں نماز شب پڑھ چکا تو وہ دوبارہ میرے پاس آئے اور کہا :تم ابھی تک گئے نہیں؟ میں نے عرض کی : ابھی تک مجھے راستہ معلوم نہیں ہوا!

EnglishDescription: The man then came to me again and said, “Have you not yet set forth?” I answered that I would stay there up to the morning. The man said, “Now, I will take you to the caravan.” He rode on a donkey, put his shovel on his shoulder, and asked me to sit behind him on the back of the animal. As I did, I pulled the rein on my horse, but it refused to move. The man then asked me to give him that rein. As I did, the man took the rein in his right hand, the shovel in his left hand, and began proceeding forward. The horse was easily led by the man.
RUrduDescription: Woh shakhs phir aaya aur kaha, “Tum ab tak gaye nahi?” Mai ne kaha keh “subha hotey hi mai yahan se chala jaawunga.” Us shakhs ne kaha, “Ab mai tumhe tumhare kaafileh tak poonchaaunga.” Woh ghade (donkey) par sawaar huwa aur apni kulhaadi ko khaande (shoulder) par rakkha, aur mujhe apne peeche baiytne ke liye kaha. Jaise mai sawaar huwa mai ne apne ghode ki rassi ko khencha, to woh aaghe nahi badha. Us shakhs ne mujhe se sawaari ki rassi talab ki. Jaise hi mai ne usey rassi di, us ne seedhe haat mein le kar aaghe ki taraf badh ne lagha aur ghoda bhi aaraam se chalne laga.
UrduDescription: تو فرمایا : زیارت جامعہ پڑھو، لیکن مجھے زیارت جامعہ یاد نہ تھی اور کئی مرتبہ زیارت کو آنے کے باوجود وہ اب بھی مجھے یاد نہیں ہے، لیکن اس وقت میں نے زیارت جامعہ زبانی پڑھنا شروع کر دی۔

EnglishDescription: The man then put his hand on my knee and asked, “Why are you all not offering Namaz Shab, Namaz Shab, Namaz Shab?” The man repeated it 3 times.
RUrduDescription: Phir us shakhs ne apna haath mere zaanu par rakkha aur pooncha, “Tum sab log Namaze Shab, Namaze Shab, Namaze Shab kyu’n nahi padhte?” 3 martaba us shakhs ne dauraaya.
UrduDescription: وہ کچھ دیر بعد پھر میرے پاس آئے اور فرمایا : تم ابھی تک گئے نہیں اور اس جگہ بیٹھے ہو؟! اس مرتبہ مجھے بے اختیار رونا آگیا اور عرض کی : ابھی تک مجھے راستے کا علم نہیں ہوا، تو آپ نے فرمایا کہ زیارت عاشورا پڑھو اور مجھے یہ زیارت بھی یاد نہ تھی اور نہ ہی اب یاد ہے۔ تا ہم میں نے یہ زیارت زبانی پڑھنا شروع کر دی اور اس کے ساتھ صلوات، لعنت اور دعائے علقمہ بھی پڑھ ڈالی۔

EnglishDescription: He then said, “Why are you deserting the Ziyarat Ashura, Ziyarat Ashura, Ziyarat Ashura?” He also repeated it 3 times.
RUrduDescription: Phir us ne kaha, “Tum Ziyarate Ashura, Ziyarate Ashura, Ziyarate Ashura ko kyu’n tarq karte ho?” yeh bhi us ne 3 martaba dauraaya.
UrduDescription: آپ پھر وہاں آئے اور فرمایا : تم ابھی تک گئے نہیں ہو؟ تو میں نے عرض کی: میں سورج نکلنے تک یہیں رہوں گا! آپ نے فرمایا: میں تمہیں قافلے تک پہنچا آتا ہوں تو آپ خچر پر سوار ہو کر بیلچہ کندھے پر رکھے ہوئے میرے قریب آگئے اور فرمایا: میرے پیچھے سوار ہو جاؤ اور میں آپ کے پیچھے سوار ہو گیا اور اپنے گھوڑے کی لگام ہاتھ میں لے لی، میں نے اسے آگے چلانے کی بہت کوشش کی مگر وہ رکا رہا، اس پر آپ نے بیلچہ بائیں کندھے پر رکھا اور میرے گھوڑے کی لگام پکڑ کر اسے چلایا تو وہ چلنے لگا۔

EnglishDescription: He then said, “Why are you not saying Ziyarat Jame’a Kabir, Ziyarat Jame’a Kabir, Ziyarat Jame’a Kabir?” He also repeated it 3 times.
RUrduDescription: Phir us ne kaha, “Tum Ziyarate Jame’a Kabir, Ziyarate Jame’a Kabir, Ziyarate Jame’a Kabir kyu’n nahi padhte?” yeh bhi us ne 3 martaba dauraaya.
UrduDescription: چلتے چلتے آپ نے اپنا ہاتھ میری ران پر رکھا اور فرمایا : تم نافلۂ تہجد کیوں نہیں پڑھتے؟ پھر تین بار فرمایا: نافلہ نافلہ نافلہ۔ پھر فرمایا کہ تم زیارت عاشورا کیوں نہیں پڑھتے اور تین بار فرمایا:عاشورا‘ عاشورا‘ عاشورا۔ پھر فرمایا : زیارت جامعہ کیوں نہیں پڑھتے اور تین بار فرمایا: جامعہ جامعہ جامعہ۔

EnglishDescription: The man was wandering in that area. Suddenly, he turned his face backward and said, “These are your companions. They are approaching the river to perform the ritual ablution for the Dawn Prayer.” Immediately, I rode off the donkey and tried to ride on my horse, but I could not. Noticing so, the man rode off his animal, put the shovel in the snow, helped me ride on the horse, and turned the horse’s face towards the direction where my companions were.
RUrduDescription: Woh shakhs safar kar raha tha. Achaanak, woh peeche muda aur kaha, “Yeh rahe tumhare saathi. Woh dariya ki taraf wuzu ke liye ja rahe hai Namaze Subh ke liye.” Fauran mai sawaari se utra aur apne ghode par sawaar hone gaya magar ho na saka. Yeh dekhte huwe woh shakhs apni sawaari par se utra, apni kulhaadi ko barfi zameen (snow) par rakkha aur madad ki meri ghode par sawaar hone par, aur mere ghode ka rukh kiya mere saathiyo’n ki taraf.
UrduDescription: آپ سفر طے کرنے میں خچر کو اس قدر تیز چلارہے تھے کہ تھوڑی ہی دیر میں گردن موڑ کر مجھ سے فرمایا : وہ دیکھو تمھارے قافلے والے نہر پر اترے ہوئے ہیں اور نمازِ فجر کے لئے وضو کر رہے ہیں۔ میں نے آپ کی سواری سے اتر کر اپنے گھوڑے پر سوار ہونا چاہا تو سوار نہ ہو سکا،آپ نے اپنی سواری سے اتر کر اپنا بیلچہ برف میں گاڑدیا اور مجھے گھوڑے پر سوار کرادیا۔

EnglishDescription: Only at that moment, I began to think and ask myself who that man was, how he could speak Persian in this region of Christian Turks, and how he could make me join my companions in such a short period of time. I turned my face back but I could not find anyone or any trace. Then, I joined my companions. This incident underlines the extraordinary importance of Ziyarat Ashura.
RUrduDescription: Usi waqt mai soch ne laga keh yeh kaun shakhs hai jo Kurdis Isaaiyo’n ke mulk mein faarsi zabaan bolta hai, aur woh kaise mujhe kam waqt mein mere saathiyo’n se mila diya. Mai ne jab mood kar dekha to woh nazar nahi aaya aur na hi uske nishaan nazar aaye. Phir mai apne saathiyo’n se ja mila. Is waaqeya se Ziyarate Ashura ki khaas ehmiyat ka pata chalta hai.
UrduDescription: اس کے ساتھ ہی اس کا رخ قافلے کی طرف موڑدیا اس وقت میں دل ہی دل میں کہہ رہا تھا کہ یہ بزرگ کون ہیں جو مجھ سے فارسی زبان میں گفتگو کرتے رہے جب کہ اس علاقہ میں ترکی زبان بولنے والوں کے علاوہ کوئی نہیں رہتا اور یہاں اکثر عیسائی لوگ رہائش پذیر ہیں۔ پھر یہ کہ انہوں نے اتنی جلدی قافلے تک پہنچا دیا ہے‘ میں نے انہیں خیالوں میں جو پیچھے مڑ کر دیکھا تو وہاں کوئی بھی نظر نہ آیا اور نہ ان کا کوئی نشان دکھائی دیا‘اس کے بعد میں قافلے والوں سے جا ملا۔

EnglishTitle: Repealing of chastisement by Ziyarat’e Ashura…
RUrduTitle: Ziyarat’e Ashura se Azaab dur hota hai…

EnglishDescription: Marhoom Mohaddis Noori (r) narrates an incident from Al-Haaj Mulla Hasan Yazdi, who in turn narrates about Mohammed Ali Yazdi who used to spend his nights in worship in a particular place in Yazd known as "Mazaar" where many prominent and pious scholars are buried. He had a neighbor who was his good friend. Both of them had grown up and studied together. His friend started doing the work of "Ghassari" (tax collection). Few years later this friend died and was buried in this very ’Mazaar’ where Janab Mohammed Ali used to spend his nights in worship.
RUrduDescription: Marhoom Mohaddis Noori riwaayat karte hai Al-Haaj Mulla Hasan Yazdi se, jo nakl karte hai Muhammad Ali Yazdi se jo saari raat ibaadat karte thay yazd mein ek khaas maqaam par jise “Mazaar” kehte hai, jahan par kahin jaiyyad aur buzurg ulma dafn hai. Unka ek padosi’n tha, jo ek behtareen dost bhi tha. Dono saath mein pale-badhe aur taalim bhi saath mein haasil ki. Unka dost zakaat wasuli (tax collector) ka kaam karta tha. kuchh saal baad unka woh dost mar gaya aur woh is Mazaar ke ilaake mein dafn huwa jahan par Janab Muhammad Ali raat ko ibaadat kiya karte thay.

EnglishDescription: One month after his friend’s demise, Janab Mohammed Ali saw him in a dream. His friend was looking very handsome and smart. Janab Mohammed Ali asked him ’I very well know the kind business in which you were involved. There was nothing in that business that you did hiddenly or openly that would merit this great reward that you have received. In fact, nothing except punishment could be expected from the kind of work you used to do. Then how did you receive this great position and reward?’ The friend replied ’You are absolutely right. In fact, from the time I died, till yesterday I was involved in a painful chastisement. But yesterday, the wife of Ustad Ashraf Haddad (blacksmith) died and was buried a few meters away from here. Last night Imam Husain (a) came to visit her thrice. On the third visit, he (a) ordered that the punishment from the entire graveyard be removed. Hence, now you see me living a life of easy and comfort.’
RUrduDescription: Jab unka dost marne ke 1 mahine baad Janab Muhammad Ali ke khwaab mein aaya to unho ne apne dost ko bohot haseen o jamil paaya. Muhammad Ali ne apne dost se pooncha, “Mujhe achchi tarah pata hai keh jo kaam-kaaj tum karte thay chhup kar ya khul kar us mein koi fazilat nahi, keh jis se tum ko yeh maqaam haasil ho. Aise kaam mein azaab ke alaawa kuchh nazar nahi aata. To phir kaise tum ko yeh maqaam aur neymat mili?” Dost ne kaha, “Tum sachh bol rahe ho. Haqeeqatan jab se mai mara tha tab se kal tak mai shadeed azaab mein tha. Magar kal jab Ustaad Ashraf Haddad (Luhaar) ki biwi ka intekaal huwa aur usey yahan kuchh duri par dafn kiya gaya, to kal raat Imam Husain (a) uski qabr par 3 martaba aaye. Jab 3rd martaba aaye, to unho ne hukm diya keh is qabrastaan se azaab ko utha liya jaaye. To ab tum mujhe aasaani aur aaraam ki zindagi mein dekh rahe ho.

EnglishDescription: Aqa Mohammed Ali got up with a start from his sleep. He did not know who Ashraf Haddad was nor did he know where he lived. He went to the iron market, inquired about him and reached his house. Janab Mohammed Ali asked him ’Did you have a wife?’ He replied ’Yes. She died last night and I buried her at so and so place’ Janab Mohammed Ali asked him ’Had she gone for the Ziyarat of Imam Husain (a)?’ The man replied ’No. But why are you asking me all this?’ Janab Mohammed Ali narrated to him the dream that he had seen. After hearing the entire incident, the blacksmith said ’My wife was very particular and regular in reciting Ziyarate Ashura.’ (Daarus Salaam, Mohaddis Noori (r), vol. 2, p.268; Shifa al-Sudur, p. 52 - Persian Translation; Mafaateeh al-Jenan)
RUrduDescription: Aqa Muhammad Ali khwaab se be-daar huwe. Unhe pata nahi tha keh Ashraf Haddad (Luhaar) kaun hai aur kaha’n rehta hai. Woh lohe ke bazaar mein pohonche aur uske ghar ka pata daryaaft kar ke uske ghar poonche. Janab Muhammad Ali ne pooncha. “Kya tumhari biwi thi?” Usne kaha, “Haa’n, kal raat hi mein uska intekaal ho gaya aur mai ne usey Mazaar mein dafn kiya.” Janab Muhammad Ali ne pooncha, “Kya woh Imam Husain (a) ki ziyarat par gayi thi?” Us shakhs ne kaha, “Nahi, magar aap mujh se yeh sawaal kyu kar rahe hai?” Janab Muhammad Ali ne apna saara khwaab bayaan kiya. Yeh soon kar Luhaar ne kaha, “Meri biwi rozana Ziyarate Ashura padhti thi.” (Daarus Salaam, Mohaddis Noori (r), vol. 2, p.268; Shifa al-Sudur, p. 52 - Persian Translation; Mafaateeh al-Jenan)

EnglishTitle: Ziyarat’e Ashura in the light of traditions…
RUrduTitle: Ziyarat’e Ashura Ahadees ki roshni mein…

EnglishDescription: Alqamah bin Mohammed ibn Hazrami (r) narrates ’I requested Imam Baqir (a) to teach me a dua that I should recite on the day of Ashura after the reciting the Ziyarat of Imam Husain (a), from near his (a) grave or from a distance.’ Imam (a) replied, ’O Alqamah, whenever you wish to recite the Ziyarat of Imam Husain (a), you first perform 2 rakat namaz. Then turn towards the grave of Imam Husain (a) and while pointing towards it, you first say ’Salam’ and then ’Takbir’. Thereafter, you recite Ziyarate Ashura. If you do so, then as if you have recited the supplication read by the angels while visiting the grave of Imam Husain (a). Moreover, when you recite this Ziyarat, then…
RUrduDescription: Alqamah ibne Muhammad ibne Hazrami (r) riwaayat karte hai keh, “Mai ne Imam Baqir (a) se iltemaas ki keh, mujhe ek aisi dua taalim kare keh jo mai Roze Ashura Imam Husain (a) ki ziyarat ke baad nazdik ya dur se padh saku.” Imam (a) ne farmaya, “Ay Alqamah jab kabhi tum chaaho Imam Husain (a) ki ziyarat padhna, to pehle 2 rakat namaz adaa karo. Phir Imam Husain (a) ki qabr ka rukh karo aur unki taraf ishaara karte huwe pehle “Salaam” aur phir “Takbir” kaho. Uske baad Ziyarate Ashura padho’n. Agar aisa tum ne kiya to goya tum ne is tarah padha jaise farishte Imam Husain (a) ki ziyarat padhte hai. Mazeed yeh keh jab tum ziyarat padhte ho to…

EnglishDescription: a) Allah will increase your grade 10,00,000 times.
RUrduDescription: a) Allah (swt) tumhare darje ko 10,00,000 martaba buland karega.

EnglishDescription: b) You will be included among those people who were martyred along with Imam Husain (a).
RUrduDescription: b) Tum un logo’n mein shumaar kiye jaaonge, jo Imam Husain (a) ke saath shaheed huwe.

EnglishDescription: c) On the day of Judgment, you will be included among the martyrs.
RUrduDescription: c) Roze qayaamat, tum shaheedo’n mein shumaar kiye jaaonge.

EnglishDescription: d) You will get the reward of all the Prophets (a) who have visited the grave of Imam Husain (a) from the day that he (a) was martyred till date. (Kaamil al-Ziyarat, Ibn Qoolwayh, Chp.71, pg.194)
RUrduDescription: d) Tum ko woh saare paighambaro’n ka ajr milega jo Imam Husain (a) ki ziyarat par gaye huwe thay, jab se unki shahadat huwi thi us din se aaj tak ka. (Kaamil al-Ziyarat, Ibn Qoolwayh, Chp.71, pg.194)

EnglishDescription: At the end of this tradition, Imam Baqir (a) says, ’O Alqamah, if you can, then recite this Ziyarat every day send salutations to Imam Husain (a). You will get all the rewards (of the one who recites it on the day of Ashura).’
RUrduDescription: Is hadees ke aakhri mein, Imam Baqir (a) ne farmaya, “Ay Alqamah, agar ho sake’n to is ziyarat ko rozana padho. Tum ko woh saara ajr milega (jo Roze Ashura is ziyarat ko padhta hai).”

EnglishDescription: Imam Sadiq (a) says: ’The one who visits the grave of Imam Husain (a) on the day of Ashura or spends the night near his (a) grave, then it is as if he attained martyrdom along with him (a).’ (Kaamil al-Ziyarat, Chp.71, Pg.191)
RUrduDescription: Imam Sadiq (a) farmaate hai, “Jo koi Qabre Imam Husain (a) ki ziyarat par Roze Ashura ya raat unki qabr ke paas basar karta hai, to aisa hai keh us ne Imam Husain (a) ke saath shahadat paayi.” (Kaamil al-Ziyarat, Chp.71, Pg.191)

EnglishDescription: Imam Sadiq (a) informs, ’The one who does the Ziyarat of Imam Husain (a) on the day of Ashura, then paradise is obligatory upon him.’ (Behaar al-Anwaar, Vol.101, Pg.104)
RUrduDescription: Imam Sadiq (a) ne farmaya. “Jo koi Imam Husain (a) ki ziyarat Roze Ashura kare, to Jannat us par laazim hai.” (Behaar al-Anwaar, Vol.101, Pg.104)

EnglishDescription: Imam Sadiq (a) mentions, ’The one who visits the grave of Imam Husain (a) knowing his right (of Imamate) then it is as if he has done the Ziyarat of Allah on the Arsh.’ (Kaamil al-Ziyarat, Chp.71, Pg.192)
RUrduDescription: Imam Sadiq (a) ne farmaya, “Agar koi Qabre Imam Husain (a) ki ziyarat kare unke haq ko pehchaante huwe, to woh aisa hai keh, jaise us ne arsh par Allah (swt) ki ziyarat ki.” (Kaamil al-Ziyarat, Chp.71, Pg.192)

EnglishDescription: Abdullah bin Fazl narrates, ’Once I was in the presence of Imam Sadiq (a) when a person from the city of Toos came to visit him (a). He asked. ’O son of the Messenger of Allah (s)! What do you have to say for the one who visits the grave of Aba Abdillah (a) on the day of Ashura?’ Imam (a) replied, ’O Toosi! The one who visits the grave of Aba Abdillah (a) while he acknowledges that his (a) obedience is obligatory from the side of Allah, then all his past and future sins shall be forgiven. He will be granted the right of intercession for 70 sinners. There will be no haajaat that he shall ask from Allah near the grave of Aba Abdillah (a) except that it shall be accepted.’ (Behaar al-Anwaar, Vol.101, Pg.23)
RUrduDescription: Abdullah ibne Fazl riwaayat karte hai, “Mai ek baar Imam Sadiq (a) ki khidmat mein tha, jab ek shakhs shaher’e Toos se Imam (a) ki mulaqaat ke liye aaya. Usne pooncha, “Ay Rasool-e-Khuda (s) ke farzand, Tum uske baare mein kya kehte ho jo Roze Ashura Imam Husain (a) ki ziyarat kare?” Imam (a) ne farmaya, “Ay Toosi! Jo koi Qabre Aba Abdillah (a) ki ziyarat Roze Ashura kare, yeh jaante huwe keh unki ita’at waajib hai Allah (swt) ki taraf se, to uske agle peechle saare gunah bakhsh diye jaayenge. Usey 70 gunah-gaar ko shafa’at karne ka haq bhi diya jaayega. Uski koi aisi haajat nahi hogi, jo us ne Allah (swt) se Aba Abdillah (a) ki qabr par talab ki ho, siwaye uske keh woh qabool ho.” (Behaar al-Anwaar, Vol.101, Pg.23)

EnglishTitle: Door of Imam Husain (a) is a Universal Door of Mercy…
RUrduTitle: Dar’e Imam Husain (a), dar’e rehmat hai qaayenaat mein…

EnglishDescription: In the sermon of ’Shabaniyyah’, Holy Prophet (s) while explaining the greatness of the month of Ramazan says ’… In it the doors of paradise are open…’ Elucidating this statement of Holy Prophet (s), Shaikh Jafar Shushtry (r) brings forth a very subtle point. About the ’doors of paradise’ he says ’If you are unable to reach the ’doors of paradise’ (ie. you are unable to enter paradise due to your bad actions) then there is yet another door that leads to paradise. No one is deprived of it and it is very easy to reach that gate. Anyone, wherever he is or in whatever condition he is in, can reach to the infinite mercy of Allah through this door. Do you want to know the name of this door so that you can enter paradise? It is door of Imam Husain (a).
RUrduDescription: Khutba Shabaniyya mein Rasoole Khuda (s) jab Mahe Ramazan ki fazilat bayaan farma rahe thay keh “…Is mahine mein Jannat ke darwaaze khol diye jaate hai…” Rasoole Khuda (s) ke is jumle ki wazaahat karte huwe, Shaikh Jafar Shushtry chand nuqte bayaaan farmaate hai. “Jannat ke darwaaze” ke baare mein “Agar tum Jannat ke darwaaze tak nahi poonch paaye” (yaani agar tum apne gunaho’n ki wajah se, Jannat mein daakhil nahi ho sake’n) to phir ek aur darwaaza hai jo Jannat ki taraf le jaata hai. Koi bhi na-muraad nahi huwa aur is dar par pohonch na bohot aasaan hai. Koi bhi, kidhar bhi ho ya kisi bhi haal mein ho, woh is darwaaze se azeem rehmate Ilaahi tak pohonch sakta hai. Kya tum jaanna chahte ho woh dar kaun sa hai, ta-keh tum Jannat mein daakhil ho sako’n?”

EnglishDescription: And the way to reach it is through humility and mourning for Aba Abdillah (a)’ Shaikh continues, ’If you cannot find the path to paradise through repentance (as there are conditions for repentance and our bad actions act as hurdles) then the love of Imam Husain (a) is an enormous grace. Regardless of the extent of a person’s sins and his non-repentance (in the true sense), yet no one is turned away from paradise because of this bounty (of the love of Imam Husain (a)) he will not be deprived of Allah’s compassion.’ (Ziyarate Ashura by Nasir Rashty, p. 35)
RUrduDescription: Woh Imam Husain (a) ka dar (door) hai. Aur is tak pohonch ne ke liye tawaazo’n (humbleness) aur Aba Abdillah (a) ka gham dil mein ho.” Shaikh phir farmaate hai. “Agar maafi ke zariye tumhe Jannat ka raasta nahi milta (jab keh maafi ke kuchh sharaayat hai aur dua ki qabooliyat mein, hamare gunah rukaawat hote hai), to Imam Husain (a) ki mohabbat azeem wasila hai. Je-chaaye keh Insaan ke gunah kitne hi ziyadah kyu’n na ho aur woh maafi talab na kare (sahih maano mein), phir bhi koi Jannat se paltaaya nahi jaayega aur rehmate Ilaahi se maayoos nahi hoga is neymat (mohabbat Imam Husain (a)) ki wajah se.” (Ziyarate Ashura by Nasir Rashty, Pg.35)

EnglishDescription: O Allah! For the sake of Imam Husain (a), you grant me honor and nobility near You in this world and the Hereafter!
RUrduDescription: Ay Allah! Imam Husain (a) ke sadaqe mein, Tu mujhe izzat aur azmat ataa kar Apne paas is duniya mein aur aakherat mein bhi.

EnglishDescription: Imam Baqir (a) said, Whoever visits Husain ibn Ali (a) on the 10th of Muharram (Ashura Day) and remains there weeping, will meet Almighty Allah on the day of meeting Him having the reward of 2000 times of going on hajj, 2000 times of going on umrah, as well as the reward of one who has participated with the Holy Messenger of Allah and with the Holy Imams in 2000 campaigns.
RUrduDescription: Imam Baqir (a) se riwaayat hai keh aap (a) ne farmaaya jo shakhs 10th Muharram ke din Imam Husain (a) ki ziyarat par jaaye aur wahan girya kare to roze qayaamat jab woh Khuda se mulaqaat karega to usey 2000 Hajj, 2000 Umrah, 2000 ghazwah ke sawaab se maala maal hoga. Yeh us shakhs jaisa sawaab hoga keh jis ne Hajj, Umrah aur jehaad Hazrat Rasoole Khuda (s) aur Aimma (a) ke saath mil kar kiya ho.
UrduDescription: شیخ ابو جعفر طوسیؒ نے کتاب مصباح میں کچھ اس طرح فرمائی ہے : محمد ابن اسماعیل ابن بزیع نے صالح ابن عقبہ سے اس نے اپنے باپ سے اور اس نے امام محمد باقر ؑ سے روایت کی ہے کہ آپ نے فرمایا: جو شخص دسویں محرم کے دن امام حسینؑ کی زیارت کرے اور اس کے ساتھ وہاں گر یہ بھی کرے تو روزِ قیامت وہ خدا سے ۲۰۰۰ حج، ۲۰۰۰ عمرہ اور ۲۰۰۰ غزوہ کے ثواب کے ساتھ اس شخص کے مثل جس نے حج،عمرہ اور جہادحضرت رسول اﷲؐ اور ائمہ طاہرینؑ کے ساتھ مل کر کیا ہو ملاقات کرے گا۔

EnglishDescription: As for such people who cannot visit the shrine, they may come out to a wasteland or go up a high roof in their houses, wave towards Imam Husain (a) with greeting, invoke earnestly curses on those who killed him, and then offer a 2 rakat namaz. They may do so before midday. They may then mourn and weep for Imam Husain (a) and order those who live with them in their houses to weep for him, unless they fear for themselves from certain individuals who live with them. They may hold mournful ceremonies in their houses and show grief for him. They may also console one another on this terrible occasion of Imam Husain’s sufferings. If they do all that, I myself guarantee for them to grant the rewards that I have mentioned beforehand.
RUrduDescription: Aise log jo dur-daraas mein rehte hai jin ke liye Ashur ke din Karbala aana mumkin na ho to us surat mein unko chahiye ke sehra mein chale jaaye ya apne ghar ki chhat par chade aur Hazrat ki taraf ishaara karte huwe salaam kare aur aap (a) ke qaatilo’n par laanat bheje phir 2 rakat namaz padhe aur yeh amal din ke pehle hisse mein (yaani waqte Zohr se pehle) baja laaye, baad mein Imam Husain (a) par girya kare. Neez ghar mein jo afraad ho agar un se taqiyya na karna ho to unhe bhi kahe keh woh girya kare. Is tarah woh apne ghar mein soug-waari aur girya o zaari ki surat banaaye aur Hazrat ke masaaeb par ba-awaaz buland rotey huwe woh log ek dusre se taaziyat kare to mai Khuda ki taraf se un logo’n ke liye zaamin hu keh agar woh is tarah amal kare to un ko bhi wohi sawaab milega.
UrduDescription: پھر امامؑ نے فرمایا: جس کے لئے عاشورہ کے دن مزار امام حسینؑ کی زیارت کو آنا ممکن نہ ہو، اس صورت میں وہ شخص صحرا میں چلا جائے یا اپنے گھر کی سب سے اونچی چھت پر چڑھے اور حضرتؑ کی طرف اشارہ کرتے ہوئے سلام کرے اور آپ کے قاتلوں پر جتنی ہو سکے لعنت بھیجے پھر ۲ رکعت نماز پڑھے اور یہ عمل دن کے پہلے حصے میں زوال سے قبل بجا لائے بعد میں امام حسینؑ کے لئے روئے اور فریاد بلند کرے نیز گھر میں جو افراد ہوں اگر ان سے تقیہ نہ کرنا ہو تو انہیں بھی کہے کہ وہ گریہ کریں۔ اس طرح وہ اپنے گھر میں سوگواری اور گریہ و زاری کی صورت بنائے اور حضرتؑ کے مصائب پر باآواز بلند روتے ہوئے وہ لوگ ایک دوسرے کو تعزیت پیش کریں تو میں خدا کی طرف سے ان لوگوں کے لئے ضامن ہوں کہ اگر وہ اس طرح عمل کریں تو ان کو بھی یہ تمام ثواب ملے گا۔

EnglishDescription: Imam Baqir (a) said: When you intend to do ziyarat of Imam Husain (a) on the day of Ashura you may offer 2 rakat namaz and then repeat the takbir (Allahu Akbar) and then say the following ziyarat. If you do so, you will have said the prayer that is said by the angels who visit Imam Husain (a). You will be also raised one hundred million ranks to join the rank of those who were martyred with him (ie. Imam Husain (a)) and you will be included with their group. You will, moreover, be awarded the reward of the visiting of all Prophets and Messengers as well as the reward of all the visitors of Imam Husain (a) since the day of his martyrdom. Peace of Allah be upon him and upon his household.”
RUrduDescription: Imam Baqir (a) farmaate hai keh jab tum Imam Husain (a) ki ziyarat ka iraada karo to 2 rakat namaz adaa karo aur baad mein takbir (Allahu Akbar) kaho aur phir yeh ziyarat ko padhoge to be-shak tum ne un alfaaz mein dua ki hai keh jin alfaaz mein woh farishte dua karte hai jo Imam Husain (a) ki ziyarat karne aate hai. Chunaan-che Khuda tumhaare liye 10,00,000 darje likkhega aur tum us shakhs ke maanind hoge jo Hazrat ke ham-raah shaheed huwa ho aur tum uske darjaat mein hisse daar ban jaawoge, neez tum un afraad mein shumaar kiye jaawoge jo Imam Husain (a) ke saath shaheed huwe hai. Neez tumhare liye har Nabi wa Rasool aur Imam mazloom ke har us zaaer ka sawaab likkha jaayega jis ne us din se keh jab se aap shaheed huwe hai aap (a) ki ziyarat ki ho. Aap par salaam ho aur aap ke khaandaan par.
UrduDescription: امام باقرؑ نے فرمایا : اے علقمہ! جب تم امام حسینؑ کی زیارت کا ارادہ کرو تو ۲ رکعت نماز ادا کر لو اس کے بعد سلام کے لئے حضرتؑ کی طرف اشارہ کرو تو اشارہ کرتے وقت تکبیر کہنے کے بعدمندرجہ ذیل زیارت پڑھو۔ کیوں کہ جب تم نے ان الفاظ میں دعا کی ہے کہ جن الفاظ میں وہ فرشتے دعا کرتے ہیں جو امام حسینؑ کی زیارت کرنے آتے ہیں،چنانچہ خدا تمہارے لئے دس لاکھ درجے لکھے گا اور تم اس شخص کے مانند ہو گے جو حضرتؑ کے ہمراہ شہید ہوا ہو اور تم اس کے درجات میں حصہ دار بن جاؤ گےنیز تم ان افراد میں شمار کیے جاؤ گے جو امام حسینؑ کے ساتھ شہید ہوئے ہیں نیز تمہارے لئے ہر نبی و رسول اور امام مظلوم کے ہر اس زائر کا ثواب لکھا جائے گا جس نے اس دن سے کہ جب سے آپ شہید ہوئے ہیں آپ کی زیارت کی ہو۔ آپ پر سلام ہواور آپ کے خاندان پر۔
